steps.action: Tamzara is an Armenian folk dance native to the with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: Tamzara is an Armenian folk dance native to the with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: In Armenia the dance originally had a ritual cha
steps.text: In Armenia the dance originally had a ritual character, it was a wedding song and dance.

steps.action: Now "Tamzara" has lost its former ritual signifi
steps.text: Now "Tamzara" has lost its former ritual significance, when it was performed during almost all community events and parties.

steps.action: It is today performed by Armenians, Assyrians, A
steps.text: It is today performed by Armenians, Assyrians, Azerbaijanis (in the regions of Sharur, Nakhchivan and parts of Iranian Azerbaijan), Greeks and Turks.

steps.action: In post-Soviet Armenia, tamzara dance is gaining
steps.text: In post-Soviet Armenia, tamzara dance is gaining more and more popularity among all strata of the population.

steps.action: This dance was also especially popular in the fo
steps.text: This dance was also especially popular in the formerly Armenian-populated regions of Erzincan, Erzurum, Kigi, Arapgir, Harput, and Malatya.

steps.action: There are many versions of Tamzara, with slightl
steps.text: There are many versions of Tamzara, with slightly different music and steps, coming from the various regions and old villages in the Armenian Highlands.

steps.action: According to Folk Dance Federation of California
steps.text: According to Folk Dance Federation of California, Inc (July, 2001) Tamzara is performed by Armenians not only in Armenia, also in the countries of Armenian Diaspora.

steps.action: The meaning of this dance, which is famous in th
steps.text: The meaning of this dance, which is famous in the villages of Charchibogan, Chomakhtur and other villages of Nakhchivan's Sharur region, is "Gizili tanbatan" (half golden) in word-by-word translation.

steps.action: Tamzara is included to the repertoire of the fol
steps.text: Tamzara is included to the repertoire of the folklore dancing collectives respectively.

steps.action: The women dancing used to dress luxuriously and
steps.text: The women dancing used to dress luxuriously and adorn themselves with golden accessories, including rings, ear-rings, bracelets, chains and others.

steps.action: In 2018, Azerbaijani-style Tamzara, along with k
steps.text: In 2018, Azerbaijani-style Tamzara, along with kochari, was inscribed into the UNESCO List of Intangible Cultural Heritage with the Yalli dance in need of urgent safeguarding.

steps.action: Like most folk dances of the Armenian Highlands,
steps.text: Like most folk dances of the Armenian Highlands, Tamzara is done as a line dance or circle dance, with a large group of people with interlocked pinkies.

steps.action: Tamzara is one of the most popular Armenian folk
steps.text: Tamzara is one of the most popular Armenian folk dances to have been preserved in the United States by the Armenian-American community.

steps.action: The Armenian Folk Dance Society performed at the
steps.text: The Armenian Folk Dance Society performed at the 1939 and 1964 World's Fair in New York.

steps.action: The name is believed to stem from a local story:
steps.text: The name is believed to stem from a local story: “The leader of the village offered his guests his hospitality and always called for his daughter-in-law Zara to serve them ‘tan’ (yoghurt mixed with salt and water).

steps.action: Therefore, he called to her by saying: ‘Tan, Zar
steps.text: Therefore, he called to her by saying: ‘Tan, Zara!

steps.action: ’” According to old local legends, this phrase e
steps.text: ’” According to old local legends, this phrase eventually led to the village being referred to as Tanzara.

steps.action: In addition, the melody to most Tamzaras is very
steps.text: In addition, the melody to most Tamzaras is very similar, though there are exceptions.

steps.action: However one version of the Tamzara is done by a
steps.text: However one version of the Tamzara is done by a man with one or two women standing shoulder to shoulder facing the same direction with their arms around each other's waists.
